chunk_summary: Guidance on feeding during and after illness for children 6–24 months emphasizes maintaining breastfeeding, offering nutrient-dense foods, and implementing catch-up feeding to restore growth and nutrition post-illness.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of maintaining breastfeeding and complementary feeding during illness.
Learn strategies for post-illness catch-up feeding to support recovery and growth.
Identify risk factors requiring follow-up after illness, such as weight loss or low dietary diversity.
key_facts: During illness, offer small, soft, nutrient-dense feeds like khichdi, curd rice, or mashed potato.
Post-illness catch-up feeding involves one additional nutrient-dense meal or snack daily for two weeks.
ORS should be used during diarrhea to prevent dehydration while continuing feeding.
Follow-up within 1–2 weeks is recommended for underweight or recently ill children.
Prolonged puree-only diets post-illness can delay feeding development.
reference_quote: Guidance on complementary feeding recommends giving food more often than usual and encouraging the child to eat more after recovery; a practical outpatient rule is one additional nutrient-dense meal or snack each day for about two weeks after an acute illness.
